title: dalbergia peguensis
intro: Dalbergia peguensis, also known as Myanmar rosewood or Burmese rosewood, is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. It has been utilized in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the bark and heartwood of Dalbergia peguensis are commonly used to make infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
